q: What is PrePulse in one sentence?
rubric: Preemptive, agentic-AI cybersecurity platform for SMBs / mid-market; six-agent fleet that identifies, validates, and stages defensive actions before an attack lands.

q: What does the Intelligence agent do?
rubric: Surveys the external threat landscape; pulls active campaigns by industry, checks domain breach history, probes suspicious IPs.

q: What does the Validator agent do?
rubric: Tests whether identified threats are exploitable against this customer's tech stack; queries NVD for CVEs and matches MITRE ATT&CK techniques.

q: What does the Hardening agent do?
rubric: Executes preemptive moving-target-defense actions: port rotation, certificate refresh, attack-surface reduction, identity posture tightening (all simulated in prototype).

q: What does the Investigator agent do?
rubric: Synthesizes prior agent outputs into an executive briefing; uses the deterministic posture engine; produces critical findings + recommended actions in plain English.

q: What does the Remediator agent do?
rubric: Builds a containment plan; gates destructive (high/critical) actions behind human approval; executes approved actions via the tool registry.

q: What does the Supervisor agent do?
rubric: Audits the other agents' reasoning; calls policy.check + audit.log_decision; signs off as approved/conditional/escalate.

q: How many agents are in the PrePulse fleet?
rubric: Six: Intelligence, Validator, Hardening, Investigator, Remediator, Supervisor.

q: Which agents run in parallel?
rubric: Validator and Hardening run in parallel after Intelligence completes.

q: What happens after the Investigator finishes?
rubric: Conditional route: posture < 75 → Remediator → Supervisor; posture ≥ 75 → Supervisor directly.

q: What is the posture score?
rubric: Deterministic 0–100 score computed in Python (not by the LLM); 100 baseline minus deductions for CVEs by severity, breach, active campaigns, abuse IPs; plus credit for clean intel and hardening actions; clamped [0,100].

q: What are the posture grade bands?
Why isn't the posture score computed by the LLM?
rubric: Determinism + auditability; every deduction is itemised and traceable; the engine is authoritative even if the LLM drifts.

q: What are the three demo profiles?
rubric: River City Financial Services (fintech), Greenfield Family Clinic (healthcare), ShopLocal Marketplace (e-commerce).

q: What's the expected score for River City?
rubric: About 48 (Grade D — elevated risk).

q: What's the expected score for Greenfield?
rubric: About 32 (Grade F — critical).

q: What's the expected score for ShopLocal?
rubric: About 72 (Grade C — healthy).

q: Why is Greenfield's score so low?
rubric: Unpatched Exchange 2019 (CVE-2023-23397), active BlackBasta ransomware campaign targeting healthcare, high-confidence abuse IP.

q: What kind of attacks does River City face?
rubric: Banking-trojan campaign (Grandoreiro), exploitable AWS Lambda CVE (CVE-2026-10234), domain in historical breaches.

q: How does the human-in-the-loop approval gate work?
rubric: Remediator pauses on high/critical actions; emits action.pending; UI renders modal; orchestrator awaits explicit approve/reject; dismissal counts as reject; configurable timeout.

q: What happens if I dismiss the approval modal?
rubric: It counts as a reject; the awaited future resolves with approved=False; scan continues to Supervisor.

q: What is the input validation layer?
rubric: Regex-based prompt-injection detector on profile fields; returns HTTP 400 with input_validation_failed before any LLM call.

q: How does PrePulse maintain reasoning transparency?
rubric: Every LLM call, tool call, agent transition emits a typed event to the in-memory bus; trace endpoint serves the full event stream as NDJSON, downloadable from the UI.

q: What is simulated in the prototype?
rubric: Hardening, Remediator, and Supervisor action tools (firewall, IAM, EDR, ticketing, email) — log to audit store, return shaped result with simulated:true; no real infrastructure mutation.

q: What is real in the prototype?
rubric: LLM reasoning (Claude Sonnet 4.6), MITRE ATT&CK retrieval, threat-intel APIs (live mode is one env flag away), all schemas + governance design.

q: Which LLM does PrePulse use?
rubric: Primary: claude-sonnet-4-6 via langchain-anthropic. Optional fallback: gpt-4o.

q: What threat-intel feeds does PrePulse consume?
rubric: OTX (AlienVault), HIBP (Have I Been Pwned), AbuseIPDB, NIST NVD.

q: How does PrePulse use MITRE ATT&CK?
rubric: Loads the enterprise STIX bundle (835+ techniques) into a vector store; the Validator agent retrieves the most semantically similar techniques per identified threat.

q: How are demos kept reliable?
rubric: Mock-first design (PREPULSE_LIVE=0 default); per-profile scripted fixtures; deterministic seed for the dashboard; offline-capable except for the LLM API itself.

q: What's in the dashboard?
rubric: Total scans, threats detected, actions executed, average posture, mean-time-to-contain, 30-day posture trend, severity distribution, defense-actions-by-kind, top MITRE techniques, agent utilisation.

q: How is the dashboard non-empty on first boot?
rubric: 30-day synthetic seed (~120 historical scans, deterministic random seed=42, dip days at 7/14/21) loaded by lifespan startup.

q: How does PrePulse scan history work?
rubric: /history page lists all scans (real + seeded); each has a link to the run console (/run/{id}) and the trace (/trace/{id}).

q: What does the trace endpoint provide?
rubric: Full NDJSON of every event emitted during a scan; downloadable from the UI; serves as the audit/compliance artefact.

q: Why does the chatbot exist?
rubric: Answers product questions for showcase reviewers, prospective customers, and team members; backed by a markdown knowledge base under backend/knowledge_base/.

q: How does the chatbot decide what to answer?
rubric: Layered guardrail: input-injection regex; semantic similarity gate (HuggingFace Inference API + sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2; threshold 0.25); LLM answers using retrieved KB chunks.

q: What happens to off-topic questions in the chatbot?
rubric: Hard refusal at the similarity gate (no LLM call); returns the fixed refusal sentence and is_in_scope=false.

q: Who built PrePulse?
rubric: NYU Tandon team for MG-9781 — Beyond Data: GenAI-Driven Business Strategy & Reinvention. Members: Ziwei Huang, Yunlong Chai, Xuanwei Fan, Zonghui Wu.

q: What course is PrePulse for?
rubric: MG-9781, NYU Tandon School of Engineering, Spring 2026.

q: Who is the target customer?
rubric: Small and mid-market organisations (30–500 employees), especially fintech, healthcare, e-commerce — companies with sensitive data but no dedicated SOC.

q: How is PrePulse priced (in concept)?
rubric: Outcome-based pricing (incidents averted, mean-time-to-contain) rather than per-endpoint telemetry volume.

q: How is PrePulse different from a SIEM-bolted-on copilot?
rubric: A copilot reacts to alerts a SIEM already generates; PrePulse's fleet plans, validates, and acts without waiting for the SIEM. Different layer of the stack.

q: Why an agent fleet rather than one big agent?
rubric: Observability and policy: narrow-function agents are easier to watch and cross-check; Supervisor audits each agent's output; single monolithic agent would be impossible to govern under EU AI Act.

q: How does PrePulse address the EU AI Act?
rubric: Trace transparency, deterministic scoring, human-in-the-loop on destructive actions, audit log per scan; Validator/Investigator likely high-risk classification — needs documented controls.

q: What are the four pillars of PrePulse?
rubric: Predictive threat intelligence, adversarial exposure validation (AEV), automated moving target defense (AMTD), autonomous agentic security operations.

q: What does AEV stand for?
rubric: Adversarial Exposure Validation — continuously simulating attacker behaviour to confirm which exposures are actually exploitable.

q: What does AMTD stand for?
rubric: Automated Moving Target Defense — continuously rotating attributes (ports, certs, credentials, addresses) to invalidate attacker recon.

q: Can I run PrePulse offline?
rubric: Mock mode (PREPULSE_LIVE=0) is the default; threat-intel API calls served from per-profile fixtures; only the Anthropic API needs internet.

q: How does the Remediator know which actions need approval?
rubric: Severity ∈ {critical, high} OR explicit requires_approval=True. Low/medium auto-execute (or auto-execute when PREPULSE_AUTO_APPROVE=1).

q: What is PrePulse's value proposition for an SMB without a SOC?
rubric: Continuous monitoring + automated defense + compliance-grade audit trail at a price/complexity an SMB can absorb; outcome-based pricing aligns vendor incentive with customer security.

q: Where is the chatbot's knowledge base stored?
rubric: backend/knowledge_base/ as Markdown files; loaded once at first chat call, cached for the process lifetime; restart picks up new files.

q: How can I add new content to the chatbot?
rubric: Drop a Markdown file under backend/knowledge_base/; restart the backend; the next chat call rebuilds the embedding index.
